heading: Clarification of munition disposal authorities
label: 426e–2 Clarification of munition disposal authorities
notes: Amendments2016—Subsec. (a). , inserted “, at full Federal expense,” after “The Secretary may” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 114–322, § 1154(1)Subsec. (b). , substituted “reimbursed” for “funded”.Pub. L. 114–322, § 1154(2)
“Secretary” DefinedSecretary means the Secretary of the Army, see , set out as a note under .section 2 of Pub. L. 113–121section 2201 of this title
amendments: Amendments2016—Subsec. (a). , inserted “, at full Federal expense,” after “The Secretary may” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 114–322, § 1154(1)Subsec. (b). , substituted “reimbursed” for “funded”.Pub. L. 114–322, § 1154(2)

children.text: the Secretary has carried out a project under civil works authority of the Secretary that includes placing sand on a beach; and

as a result of the project described in paragraph (1), military munitions that were originally released as a result of Department of Defense activities are deposited on the beach, posing a threat to human health or the environment.

children.heading: Response action funding
children.label: b Response action funding
children.text: A response action described in subsection (a) shall be reimbursed from amounts made available to the agency within the Department of Defense responsible for the original release of the munitions.
